insight:
Yesterday was a solid rest day with your body showing great recovery signs. With 4,619 steps and no formal training, you burned 2,323 calories - right on target. Your resting heart rate of 44 bpm shows excellent cardiovascular fitness, and that low stress level of 27/100 indicates you're managing pre-race nerves well.

Your HRV of 113ms sits beautifully within your baseline range of 94-116ms, signaling your body is recovered and ready. Combined with that stellar resting heart rate, this tells me you can stick to your planned session today without any modifications. Your nervous system is primed and responsive.

The 6.6 hours of sleep falls short of what you need this close to race day. Tonight, prioritize getting 7-8 hours - set a firm bedtime and create a wind-down routine. Good sleep will optimize your glycogen stores and keep that excellent recovery trending upward for Sunday's half marathon.
